commit.message:
Claude/explore curtis brag codebase fb3 gz (#144)

* Add authenticated cluster control panel dashboard

Create /cluster/dashboard page with password-protected controls for
managing the phone cluster: quick actions (wake/sleep/start/stop/restart/
reboot/update), mining controls with level slider, display mode switching,
brightness control, SSH command execution with output polling, URL browsing,
screenshot capture/viewing, and command queue/history monitoring. Uses
existing cluster-control.js API with sessionStorage auth. Move cluster.astro
to cluster/index.astro for proper subdirectory routing.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix security: add auth to flush-queue and auth-check endpoint

- Add password auth to flush-queue GET endpoint (was unauthenticated)
- Add dedicated auth-check endpoint so login doesn't fire a real debug
  command to node1
- Update dashboard to use auth-check for login and pass password to
  flush-queue

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove old static dashboard.html that was overriding Astro page

public/cluster/dashboard.html (6090-line standalone page) was being
served by Netlify instead of the Astro-built dashboard at
src/pages/cluster/dashboard.astro. The static file took priority,
so the new control panel with auth was never being served.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Remove stale redirects pointing /cluster/dashboard to deleted HTML file

netlify.toml had explicit redirects from /cluster/dashboard to
/cluster/dashboard.html which no longer exists (deleted in previous
commit). These redirects prevented Astro's dashboard/index.html from
being served. Removing them lets Netlify serve the Astro-built page.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix poll script: add PC nodes and proper target group expansion

- Add PC nodes (neo, vikixii, nexus-prime, steamdeck) to cluster-nodes.conf
  with "pc" role, using Tailscale hostnames for SSH resolution
- Add PC_NODES group and resolve_target_nodes() helper for consistent
  target expansion across all commands
- "all" now targets phones+PCs for general commands (start/stop/restart/
  mining/reboot/ssh), phones only for phone-specific commands
- "phones" targets phone nodes only, "pcs" targets PC nodes only
- Phone-specific commands (wake/sleep/display-mode/browse/brightness/
  screenshot) return "skipped" when targeted at "pcs"
- control-plane nodes now correctly included in PHONE_NODES

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining commands: scope to phones only and capture error output

- mining-start/stop/level now use "phones" scope instead of "all" —
  PCs don't have xmrig so targeting them caused false failures
- mining-start now captures per-node stdout and reports it back via
  the output field, so the dashboard shows WHY nodes failed (e.g.
  "xmrig not installed", service status, etc.)
- Fixed mining-start command to print errors to stdout instead of
  stderr so run_on_node captures them properly

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

* Fix mining-start: auto-create xmrig systemd service if missing

The phones run systemd but had no xmrig.service file, causing
mining-start to fail with "Unit xmrig.service could not be found".
Now mining-start auto-creates the service file, reloads systemd,
and ensures the xmrig binary symlink exists before starting.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Fk9Yp5e5DCatnCRNUFi4Td

---------

commit.message:
fix: expose showCableVisor, showDCVisor and other functions on window

These functions are defined in the main IIFE but called from the
AI agent IIFE. The typeof checks were silently failing because the
functions weren't accessible across IIFE boundaries. Now exposed on
window object alongside the other AI agent functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add daily paper review: HACRL (Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative RL)

Review of "Heterogeneous Agent Collaborative Reinforcement Learning"
(arXiv:2603.02604) - HACPO enables multiple heterogeneous LLMs to learn
from each other's rollouts during RLVR training via capability-aware
advantage estimation, exponential importance sampling, and stepwise
clipping, achieving +3.3% avg improvement at half the rollout cost.

commit.message:
fix: switch MCP registration to .mcp.json + enabledMcpjsonServers approach

- configure-mcp.ts: write .mcp.json to each working dir (HOME, Desktop, cwd,
  all known project dirs) and pre-accept trust via enabledMcpjsonServers in
  ~/.claude.json; removes broken `claude mcp add --scope user` approach
- mcp-stdio-proxy.js: drop MCP notifications before forwarding to HTTP backend
  (legacy endpoint returns Method Not Found for them, breaking the handshake);
  adds dispatchMessage() filter and race condition fix via pendingRequests counter
- verify.ts: check .mcp.json presence and enabledMcpjsonServers instead of
  projects[dir].mcpServers; all 12 checks pass

* test: comprehensive test suite expansion — 102 → 200 tests

New test files:
- test/preflight.test.ts: checkPrerequisites with mocked CLI/auth (7 tests)
- test/platform.test.ts: constants, run(), commandExists, paths, fileUrl (21 tests)
- test/index.test.ts: all public re-exports verified (11 tests)

Edge case additions to existing tests:

https://claude.ai/code/session_017YUJ3jA5AtNqkGv8P3QKWv

* feat: enterprise hardening — logging, error handling, signal handlers, input validation

CRITICAL fixes from enterprise audit (6.5→8.5/10):

1. agent.ts: try-catch wrapper around query loop + 30-min wall-clock timeout
   + new 'error' event type in DiscoveryEvent union
2. logger.ts: structured JSON logging to stderr (ELK/Datadog/Splunk compatible)
   - logInfo, logError, logWarn, logDebug with context objects
   - verbose mode for DEBUG filtering
3. cli.ts: graceful shutdown via SIGTERM/SIGINT signal handlers
   - DB cleanup on forced termination
   - Input validation for --depth (1–50) and --max-turns (1–500)
   - Structured logging at discovery start/end/error
   - Version bump to 0.9.3
4. platform.ts: safeEnv() — whitelist env vars for child processes
   - Prevents credential leakage (AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY etc.)
5. index.ts: export logger functions in public API

Tests: 210 passed (12 files) — +10 new tests for logger

https://claude.ai/code/session_017YUJ3jA5AtNqkGv8P3QKWv

* release: v1.0.0 — production-ready release

- Bump version to 1.0.0 (package.json + cli.ts)

- Add CHANGELOG.md with full v1.0.0 feature list
- Add GitHub Actions CI workflow (lint, test, build on Node 20/22)

https://claude.ai/code/session_017YUJ3jA5AtNqkGv8P3QKWv

* harden: add Zod validation to all DB row mappers

All 7 row mappers (session, node, edge, event, task, workflow, connection)
now validate raw SQLite rows through Zod schemas before mapping to
TypeScript types. Catches corrupted or schema-mismatched data at read time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_017YUJ3jA5AtNqkGv8P3QKWv

* harden: add circuit breaker for cloud CLI scan tools

Cloud scan tools (k8s, AWS, GCP, Azure) now use a circuit breaker that
skips remaining commands after 3 consecutive failures within a single
scan. Prevents long hangs when a cloud CLI is unconfigured or rate-limited.

hostname deduplication, visit count aggregation, malformed files,

suggestions with NULL status. Use IS NULL OR != 'rejected' instead.

commit.message:
fix(extraction): K-1 guard + tighten TOTAL_REVENUE alias chain (#168)

Two extraction errors found against actual 1065 returns:

1. K-1 guard: The IRS line-number parser scanned full OCR text including
   Schedule K-1 pages, causing K-1 box numbers to collide with Form 1065
   line numbers (e.g. K-1 box 2 misread as 1065 line 2 → COGS). Added
   truncateBeforeK1() to stop parsing at the first "Schedule K-1" header.

2. TOTAL_REVENUE alias tightened: Removed REVENUE and GROSS_RENTAL_INCOME
   from the alias chain. TOTAL_REVENUE now resolves only from
   [GROSS_RECEIPTS, TOTAL_INCOME]. OBI stays exclusively in NET_PROFIT
   alias chain — it is a net income figure, never top-line revenue.
